title: Fix Gemma 4 model ID and implement programmatic model profile switching
description:
PR #32 add configs/models/*.yaml reference files but (a) uses a fabricated model ID 'gemma-4-12b-it-bnb-4bit' that does not exist on HuggingFace, and (b) provides no code to actually switch models — users still have to manually edit config.yaml.

Tasks:
1. Fix model ID: rename configs/models/gemma4-12b.yaml → configs/models/gemma4-e4b.yaml. Correct model_id: 'unsloth/gemma-4-E4B-it-unsloth-bnb-4bit' (8B, fits on RTX 3090 24GB VRAM). Use lora_r: 16, lora_alpha: 16, lora_dropout: 0.0, max_seq_length: 4096, batch_size: 2, gradient_accumulation_steps: 4, learning_rate: 5e-5, output_dir: './models/kaya_gemma4_e4b'.
2. Add model_profiles and active_model_profile keys to config.yaml and config.docker.yaml containing the above two profiles (qwen3-14b and gemma4-e4b). Existing top-level model: and training: sections remain as the 'resolved' config.
3. Create src/config_loader.py: function load_config(path, profile_override=None) that loads YAML, deep-merges the active profile's values into model: and training: sections, and returns the resolved dict. If no active_model_profile is set, falls back to existing top-level values (backward compatible).
4. Update ALL config consumers to use load_config: src/finetuning/train.py, src/finetuning/trainer.py, src/chat/chat.py, src/chat/inference.py, src/chat/retriever.py, src/data/build_vector_db.py, run_full_pipeline.py.
5. Add --profile <name> CLI argument to run_full_pipeline.py, src/finetuning/train.py, and src/chat/chat.py. When provided, it overrides active_model_profile before resolving config.
6. Update .github/workflows/gpu-pipeline.yml: add a 'profile' string input to workflow_dispatch (default: blank = use config default). Pass it as --profile arg to the pipeline command in docker-compose override.
7. Update .github/copilot-instructions.md: add Hardware Constraints section (RTX 3090 24GB VRAM, 32GB system RAM, CUDA 12.4), Model Profiles section listing available profiles with VRAM estimates, and note that Gemma 4 E4B fits comfortably (~11GB) while the 26B-A4B MoE is excluded due to QLoRA limitations.
8. Update .github/agents/model-trainer.agent.md: add VRAM budget table for each profile (qwen3-14b ~15GB, gemma4-e4b ~11GB), and note that if training OOMs the fallback is to reduce lora_r to 8 and max_seq_length to 2048.

title: Add tests for config profile loader and model switching
description:
Add test coverage for the new model profile system added in the feature-dev task.

Tests to write (in tests/pipeline/ or tests/):
1. test_config_loader.py:
   - Profile merge: loading config with active_model_profile='gemma4-e4b' should override model.model_id, training.output_dir, lora settings correctly
   - Backward compat: config without model_profiles / active_model_profile should return existing top-level values unchanged
   - Invalid profile: load_config with profile_override='nonexistent' should raise a clear ValueError (not KeyError)
   - profile_override kwarg: explicitly passing profile_override='qwen3-14b' should override active_model_profile from file
   - Deep merge: profile values should override only specified keys; unspecified keys (e.g. lora_dropout) fall through from base config
2. Update tests/test_inference.py if needed to ensure new config loader import does not break it
3. Do not modify any production code.

Note: hardware constraint is RTX 3090 24GB VRAM — any tests that mock VRAM estimation should use 24576 MB as the hardware limit.

title: Run Gemma 4 E4B full pipeline and compare benchmarks with Qwen3-14B baseline
description:
Run the full training pipeline for both model profiles and compare benchmark results side-by-side.

This task depends on the feature-dev task (config_loader + profile system) and test-specialist task being merged first.

Steps:
1. Verify Qwen3-14B baseline:
   - Confirm PR #32 changes (unsloth 2026.4.2, renamed output dir kaya_qwen3_14b) are compatible
   - Trigger GPU pipeline with --profile qwen3-14b (or active_model_profile default)
   - Save baseline benchmark scores
2. Train Gemma 4 E4B:
   - Trigger GPU pipeline with --profile gemma4-e4b
   - Model: unsloth/gemma-4-E4B-it-unsloth-bnb-4bit, lora_r=16, seq=4096, batch=2, grad_accum=4
   - Expected VRAM: ~11GB (comfortable on RTX 3090 24GB)
   - If OOM: reduce lora_r to 8, max_seq_length to 2048
   - Run golden tests and judge-eval benchmark
3. Enable models benchmark dimension:
   - In config.yaml benchmark.dimensions, uncomment '- models'
   - Populate benchmark.models with both profile output_dirs after training: ['./models/kaya_qwen3_14b', './models/kaya_gemma4_e4b']
   - Run comparison benchmark (identity_adherence, factual_grounding, Portuguese language quality, inference speed tokens/sec, VRAM at inference)
4. Thresholds: identity_adherence >= 3.5/5, factual_grounding >= 3.0/5 for both models
5. If Gemma 4 E4B scores are below thresholds, emit a new bug task via tasks.json for the bug-fixer agent.
6. Do NOT run training commands locally — all GPU work is handled by the gpu-pipeline.yml workflow on the self-hosted RTX 3090 runner.
